channel.title: Frontion - Global News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: Italy cancels Kanye West and Travis Scott concerts over security concerns
item.description: Italian authorities banned two concerts in Reggio Emilia due to public order concerns and potential protests. West has faced cancellations across Europe following antisemitic remarks, while Scott has been under scrutiny since the Astroworld crowd crush that killed 10 people.

item.title: Truck crash kills 22 Afghan refugees returning from Pakistan
item.description: A truck carrying Afghan refugees returning from Pakistan overturned in eastern Afghanistan, killing at least 22 people including 10 children. The crash comes as millions of Afghans have returned from Pakistan and Iran following crackdowns on migrants.

item.title: Israeli troops push deeper into Lebanon as Pentagon talks begin
item.description: Israeli forces entered the southern Lebanese village of Dibbine near Marjayoun as Lebanese and Israeli military officials held their first direct talks at the Pentagon in decades. The violence continues despite a nominal ceasefire that began in April.

item.title: Spain legalizes over 500,000 undocumented immigrants in regularization program
item.description: Spain is processing over 549,000 applications to regularize undocumented immigrants by June 30, a stark contrast to US deportation policies. Prime Minister Sánchez called it an act of normalization for people already part of Spanish daily life.

item.title: Russian drone hits Romanian apartment building near Ukraine border
item.description: A Russian drone struck a residential apartment building in Romania near the Ukraine border, prompting NATO condemnation. Romania's defense ministry said the drone was likely hit by Ukrainian air defenses before crashing into the building.

item.title: Hegseth tells Asian allies US not 'turning back' but expects more defense spending
item.description: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth addressed the Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore, reassuring Asia-Pacific allies of American commitment while calling for increased defense spending from partners.

item.title: US Defense Secretary Hegseth affirms commitment to Asia at Singapore summit
item.description: Pete Hegseth told Asian allies the US is not 'turning back' on the region but expects them to boost defense spending to counter China's military buildup during remarks at the Shangri-La Dialogue.

item.title: WHO chief visits DRC Ebola epicenter as outbreak death rate reaches 30-50%
item.description: WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus arrived in the Democratic Republic of Congo as the Ebola outbreak death rate was revised to 30-50%. The outbreak in a gold mining town is spreading faster than response efforts.

item.title: Germany deploys troops to Lithuania in historic NATO move
item.description: Germany is deploying a permanent military presence in Lithuania, marking the first time German troops will be permanently stationed abroad since World War II, as NATO strengthens its eastern flank against Russia.

item.title: Japan buys $14 million in military equipment for Ukraine under NATO program
item.description: Japan purchased over $14 million in military equipment for Ukraine through a NATO trust fund program, marking an expansion of Japanese military support despite constitutional restrictions on arms exports.

item.title: Turkey sees strong potential for drone development with Japan
item.description: Turkey and Japan are exploring joint drone development opportunities, combining Turkish combat experience with Japanese technology as both nations seek to strengthen defense capabilities.

item.title: Pentagon accelerates space-based aircraft tracking with $4B SpaceX deal
item.description: The Pentagon announced a $4 billion contract with SpaceX to accelerate development of space-based systems for tracking aircraft, extending the company's growing role in defense infrastructure.

item.title: Ukraine using AI drones to strike vital Russian supply lines
item.description: Ukraine is deploying AI-enabled Hornet drones to attack Russian logistics along critical supply routes. BBC Verify confirmed at least 14 incidents of vehicles carrying fuel, food and ammunition being targeted.

item.title: Trump weighs Iran deal decision after meeting with advisers
item.description: President Trump held a Situation Room meeting with advisers on a potential Iran ceasefire deal but made no final decision. Iran stated the agreement has not been finalized and no step will be taken before the US acts.

item.title: Blue Origin New Glenn rocket explodes during Florida test
item.description: A Blue Origin New Glenn rocket exploded on the launch pad at Cape Canaveral during testing, raising questions about the company's timeline for NASA Moon missions and commercial operations.

item.title: Rwanda signs nuclear cooperation deal with Russia
item.description: Rwanda signed a nuclear cooperation agreement with Russia at the Nuclear Energy Innovation Summit in Kigali, signaling growing Russian influence in Africa as Western partnerships face questions over consistency.

item.title: Kenya court blocks US Ebola quarantine center for Americans
item.description: A Kenyan court halted plans for a US-funded Ebola quarantine center for Americans amid the DRC outbreak, raising questions about the handling of outbreak response and the role of foreign infrastructure.

item.title: Zelensky warns Russia preparing massive new strike
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky warned that Russia is preparing a large-scale new attack, as Ukrainian forces continue to target Russian supply lines with AI-enabled drones.

item.title: Chinese scientists create 'kill-them-all' drone warfare algorithm
item.description: Chinese researchers developed an algorithm for autonomous drone swarms that can independently identify and attack targets, raising concerns about AI weapons proliferation.

item.title: Polish president threatens to strip Zelensky of top state honor
item.description: Poland's president threatened to revoke a top state honor from Ukraine's Volodymyr Zelensky amid tensions between the two allies over grain imports and historical disputes.

item.title: Trump concludes Iran meeting without announcing deal decision
item.description: President Trump held a Situation Room meeting with advisers on a potential Iran ceasefire deal but made no final decision. Iran stated the agreement has not been finalized.

item.title: WHO chief visits DRC Ebola epicenter as outbreak death rate reaches 30-50%
item.description: WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus visited the Democratic Republic of Congo as the Ebola outbreak death rate was revised to 30-50%.

item.title: Rwanda-Russia nuclear deal underscores Africa's shifting power balance
item.description: Rwanda signed a nuclear cooperation agreement with Russia, advancing ambitions in technology, healthcare and diplomacy while signaling shifting alliances in Africa.

item.title: Germany deploys troops to Lithuania in historic NATO move
item.description: Germany is permanently stationing troops in Lithuania for the first time since WWII as NATO strengthens its eastern flank against Russia.
